xlang.config.locale: Override the extension's display language. Set to `auto` to follow VS Code's display language, or choose a specific language. Takes precedence over VS Code's `--locale` setting.
xlang.config.serverPath: Xlang compiler (`xlang`) executable path for LSP (diagnostics, go-to-definition, find references, formatting). Can be `xlang` (PATH lookup; auto-uses `compiler/xlang` if present in workspace), a relative workspace path (e.g. `compiler/xlang`), or an absolute path.
xlang.config.server.trace: Log level for LSP client-server communication. Set to `verbose` to inspect JSON interaction when debugging language service issues. Logs output to the **Xlang** output channel.
xlang.config.server.restartOnCrash: Auto-restart language server process on crash. When disabled, use **Xlang: Restart Language Server** command to recover.
xlang.config.format.enabled: Enable Xlang code formatting. When disabled, **Format Document** command is inactive for `.x` files.
xlang.config.format.tabSize: Spaces per indent level. Only effective when `xlang.format.insertSpaces` is `true`.
xlang.config.format.insertSpaces: Use spaces for indentation. Set to `false` for Tab indentation.
xlang.config.format.maxLineLength: Maximum characters per line. Formatter wraps at suitable positions when exceeded. Passed to `xlang --lsp` as `--max-line-length`.
xlang.config.features.codeLens: Enable inline CodeLens (▶ Run, struct/enum field count, etc.).
xlang.config.features.codeLensFunctionLa: Show `fn` / `extern fn` labels above `function` / `extern function` definition lines. Off by default; does not affect ▶ Run or struct/enum hints.
xlang.config.features.statusBar: Enable bottom-right symbol count status bar.
xlang.config.features.documentHighlight: Enable document highlight (DocumentHighlightProvider): highlights all same identifiers when cursor is on an identifier.
xlang.config.features.codeAction: Enable CodeAction (lightbulb): provides fix suggestions for parse/typeck errors.
xlang.config.features.workspaceSymbol: Enable global symbol search (Ctrl+T / Cmd+T). Scans top-level `function`/`struct`/`enum`/`trait`/`impl`/`type` declarations in workspace `.x` files. Implemented locally; does not depend on LSP `workspaceSymbolProvider`.
xlang.config.features.statusBarDiagnosti: Show current file's error and warning counts in the status bar (e.g. `· $(error) 3 · $(warning) 5`). Requires `xlang.features.statusBar` to be enabled.
xlang.config.features.statusBarLspStatus: Show LSP connection indicator in the status bar (`$(circle-slash)` when disconnected). Requires `xlang.features.statusBar` to be enabled.
xlang.config.features.codeLensRunButton: Show the `▶ Run` CodeLens above `function main` definitions. Requires `xlang.features.codeLens` to be enabled.
xlang.config.features.codeLensStructInfo: Show field/variant/method count CodeLens above `struct`/`enum`/`trait`/`impl`/`type` definitions. Requires `xlang.features.codeLens` to be enabled.
xlang.config.compiler.diagnosticLevel: Minimum diagnostic level reported by compiler. Affects diagnostic markers for `.x` files in the Problems panel.
xlang.config.compiler.extraArgs: Extra command-line arguments passed to `xlang --lsp`, e.g. `["--verbose"]`.
xlang.config.compiler.targetDir: Compiler output directory (e.g. `build/`). Relative paths are based on workspace root. Empty uses compiler default.
xlang.config.compiler.libRoots: Import path resolution library root directories, aligned with `xlang -L` layout. Used for import navigation and cross-module symbol indexing.
xlang.config.compiler.envJson: Environment variables passed to `xlang` process, e.g. `{ "XLANG_PATH": "/opt/xlang" }`.
xlang.config.build.optimizationLevel: Optimization level passed to `xlang build` (via `-O<level>`). Higher levels improve runtime performance at the cost of compile time.
xlang.config.build.optimizationLevel.0: No optimization (fastest compile, best for development)
xlang.config.build.optimizationLevel.3: Aggressive optimization (slowest compile, best runtime perf)
xlang.config.build.debugInfo: Emit debug info (passed as `-g` to `xlang build`). Enables source location mapping for use with debuggers and crash traces.
xlang.config.build.outputName: Override output binary name (passed as `-o <name>` to `xlang build`). Empty uses compiler default (typically derived from the source file or project name).
xlang.config.workspaceSymbol.maxFiles: Maximum number of `.x` files to scan for global symbol search (Ctrl+T). Raising this affects first-search latency on large workspaces. Range: 100–50000.
xlang.config.workspaceSymbol.excludePatt: Glob patterns to exclude from workspace symbol scanning. Patterns are joined with the default excludes (`node_modules`/`.git`/`out`/`dist`/`target`/`build`). Use this to skip vendored or generated code directories.
xlang.walkthrough.welcome.title: Xlang Language Development Quickstart
xlang.walkthrough.welcome.description: Quickly set up your Xlang (.x) development environment
xlang.walkthrough.openProject.title: Open Xlang Project
xlang.walkthrough.openProject.descriptio: Use **Open Folder** to open the xlang repository root. LSP requires a workspace to start diagnostics, navigation, and completion.
xlang.walkthrough.buildCompiler.title: Build Xlang Compiler
xlang.walkthrough.buildCompiler.descript: Run `make -C compiler bootstrap-driver-seed` to obtain the `compiler/xlang` binary required for LSP.
xlang.walkthrough.openXFile.title: Open .x File to Experience
xlang.walkthrough.openXFile.description: Open any `.x` file to verify syntax highlighting, folding, CodeLens, and status bar.
xlang.walkthrough.runFile.title: Run .x File
xlang.walkthrough.runFile.description: Click ▶ Run CodeLens above `function main`, or use **Xlang: Run Current .x File** from the command palette.
xlang.taskDefinition.task.description: Xlang task type: build / build-file / run / check
xlang.walkthrough.openProject.media: Press `Ctrl+K Ctrl+O` to open the project root directory (containing `compiler/`).
xlang.walkthrough.buildCompiler.media:
Run in terminal:
```
make -C compiler bootstrap-driver-seed
```
After build succeeds, `compiler/xlang` will serve as the language server.
xlang.walkthrough.openXFile.media: Try opening `compiler/src/lexer/lexer.x` or any test file under `tests/`.
xlang.walkthrough.runFile.media: CodeLens `▶ Run` appears on the `function main` definition line.
